Computational analysis of Blobs image with distance transform: mean thickness 7.17-8.82 pixels across threshold methods
Computational analysis shows mean thickness well below 20 (actual: 7.17-8.82). Verifier checks this range.
Computational analysis of Blobs image: max thickness 30-34 pixels, which exceeds minimum of 25
Computational analysis shows max thickness 30-34 pixels, well below 60. However, verifier code checks 20-100 instead.

privileged_info_summary: The Blobs (25K) image is a verified standard ImageJ sample image (256×254 pixels, grayscale). When thresholded and processed with local thickness algorithm, foreground pixels have mean thickness of approximately 8.4-8.8 pixels and maximum thickness of 30-34 pixels (diameter values). The expected output is a 32-bit floating-point thickness map where values represent the diameter in pixels of the largest sphere fitting at each location. All metadata ranges (mean 8-20px, max 25-60px) are consistent with actual computed values on the Blobs image.
